description.task: Implement a CircularQueue class using a fixed-size array with head and tail indices that wrap around. When the array is full, enqueue should throw an exception. Demonstrate isFull and isEmpty correctly reflect state.
instructions: dequeue: head=(head+1)%capacity; size--; (val is already saved before this)
After enqueue(1,2,3): size=3; after dequeue(): head advances, returns 1
After enqueue(4,5): queue wraps around; isFull=true (size==capacity=4)
description.hint: enqueue: data[tail]=val; tail=(tail+1)%capacity; size++. dequeue: head=(head+1)%capacity; size--;

description.task: Implement a MaxPriorityQueue class backed by a max-heap (int array). Support insert and extractMax. The heap invariant: parent >= children. Use swim-up on insert and sink-down on extract.
instructions: insert swim-up is already implemented — read it to understand the pattern
extractMax sink-down: int k=1; while(2*k<=size){int j=2*k; if(j<size&&heap[j]<heap[j+1]) j++; if(heap[k]>=heap[j]) break; swap(k,j); k=j;}
Heap is 1-indexed: root at index 1, children of k at 2k and 2k+1
description.hint: sink-down: int k=1; while(2*k<=size){int j=2*k; if(j<size&&heap[j]<heap[j+1])j++; if(heap[k]>=heap[j])break; swap(k,j); k=j;}

description.task: Implement a Deque class backed by a doubly linked list. Support addFirst, addLast, removeFirst, removeLast, peekFirst, and peekLast. Demonstrate using it as both a stack and a queue.
instructions: addLast(val): Node n=new Node(val); if(tail==null){head=tail=n;} else {tail.next=n; n.prev=tail; tail=n;} size++;
removeLast(): if(head==tail){int v=tail.val; head=tail=null; size--; return v;} int v=tail.val; tail=tail.prev; tail.next=null; size--; return v;
addFirst and removeFirst are already implemented as reference
As queue: addLast(1,2,3) then removeFirst three times → 1, 2, 3
As stack: addFirst(3,2,1) then removeFirst three times → 1, 2, 3
